message:
test(e2e,bd-0a8y3): fresh-corpus regression for explicit --mode hybrid lexical fail-open

The sibling test in tests/cli_robot.rs
(search_robot_meta_reports_explicit_hybrid_fail_open) exercises the
"`--mode hybrid` fails open to lexical when semantic assets are
absent" contract (commit 86c88d0b) against the committed
tests/fixtures/search_demo_data snapshot. That's good coverage for the
bundled fixture path, but it doesn't exercise the fresh-build pipeline
where a schema or indexer regression could still break the contract on
first-time corpora.

tests/e2e_lexical_fail_open.rs (new, bead 0a8y3, child of ibuuh.10):
- Seed Codex session JSONL into an isolated tempdir, scrub every
  HOME/XDG/CODEX env var so the test never touches the operator's
  real corpus, set CASS_IGNORE_SOURCES_CONFIG=1 so sources.toml is
  ignored.
- Build both the canonical DB and the lexical index from scratch, then
  run `cass search --mode hybrid --robot-meta` and assert the `_meta`
  block names the fail-open contract exactly:
    - requested_search_mode = "hybrid" (preserved intent)
    - search_mode = "lexical" (realized tier)
    - mode_defaulted = false (user explicitly passed --mode hybrid)
    - fallback_tier = "lexical"
    - semantic_refinement = false

tests/golden_robot_json.rs: cosmetic rustfmt — three regex::Regex::new
calls reflowed to single lines under the column budget. No behavior
change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
